author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! sādhu saṅge karaha vasati
jadi karmapāśa⋅bandhe, magana karaye andhe,
jadi kula⋅vihīna utpati

jadi paśu⋅pakṣī⋅kṛmi, janmiyā janmiyā bhrami,
satata karāya gatāgati
jemana temana sthāne, gṛhe vā parvate vane,
kā̐hā kene nā haya vasati

thāke jena ei sūtra, dṛḍha⋅cita ei mātra,
śrī⋅haricaraṇe rati⋅mati
ghucibe sakala dukha, pāibe aśeṣa sukha,
bujhi kara śrī⋅hari⋅bhakati

dharma⋅karma⋅jñāna⋅yoga, svarga⋅mokṣa⋅bhukti⋅bhoga,
‘kṛṣṇa⋅sevānanda’⋅ihā vine
jadi ithe kona kṣaṇa, bāndha tāya āmāya mana,
tabe jena haya ta’ maraṇe

‘rādhā’ ‘kṛṣṇa’ du’ṭī nāma, jihvā jena avirāma,
du̐hu⋅guṇa⋅līlāte śravaṇa
kahe premānanda dīne, du̐hu cintā anukṣaṇe,
rūpe jena thākaye nayana
en_translation:
O my mind! Live always in the company of the saintly. Even if you are blindly bound by the results of your past actions, and even if you are born into a family without any prestige.

Even if you are born repeatedly as an animal, a bird, or an insect, wandering constantly through the cycle of birth and death—wherever you may be, whether in a home, on a mountain, or in a forest, always seek the company of the saintly.

Let this be your only firm principle: to maintain love and devotion for the lotus feet of Lord Hari. Then all your miseries will cease, and you will attain endless happiness. Understanding this, perform devotional service to Lord Hari.

Religious duties, fruitive actions, speculative knowledge, mystic yoga, heaven, liberation, and material enjoyment—if my mind is bound to any of these for even a single moment instead of the bliss of serving Lord Kṛṣṇa, then let that moment be my death.

May the two Names "Rādhā" and "Kṛṣṇa" be upon my tongue without ceasing, and may my ears always hear of Their qualities and pastimes. Premānanda humbly says: "May I constantly meditate upon Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, and may my eyes always be fixed upon Their divine forms."
